other_observers: None; It was seen by several birders in several days that it stuck around, the finders were Tom and Mary France and Larry Griffin.
specific_location: In a marshy area on County Road 48 on the south side of the road just south of Lower Latham Reservoir.
description: It has a long thin gray bill with a black tip. A dark blue and purplish neck and head with a white chin and a yellow front of neck. It had a red eye. The color on the back was the same color as the head and neck. It had a white belly and lower breast. It also had long pinkish gray legs.Behaviors: When I saw it the bird was on the far end of the cattails. For more of the time that I watched it was actively feeding, including the dashing that is does to feed and a few times it did the dancing that Egretta occasionally does, mostly REEG. It then disappeared into the cattails.Call: Didn’t hear itPlumage: Adult Breeding
